title: Quantum Entanglement
definition: A phenomenon where particles become correlated such that the quantum state of each particle cannot be described independently, as discovered by Einstein.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Entangled particles are like two dice that always show matching numbers, no matter how far apart they are.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Quantum entanglement is a phenomenon where particles remain connected regardless of distance, challenging our understanding of locality in quantum mechanics.
difficulty_levels.expert: Entanglement challenges the very foundations of quantum mechanics by demonstrating non-locality and interdependence between particles.

extra_fields.insight: Entangled particles are mysteriously linked, defying intuition about distance and separation.
